rulings.Case Title: EUGENE PITT SHEFFIELD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Intervenors' Motion For An Order Granting Relief From The Court'S Assessment Of A "First Paper" Filing Fee To Each Intervenor; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed "First Paper" Filing Fees Be Reduced To A Total Of $410.00 For All Intervenors; Or, In The Alternative, That The Assessed "First Paper" Filing Fees Be Equitably Reduced (Added To Calendar Per Ruling At The 10/24/12 Hearing In Case #828684)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ motion for relief from the court¿s assessment of a first paper filing fee, or in the alternative, that the assessed first paper filing fees be reduced: hearing required. The hearing will be at 11:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY WAYNE KING VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 18. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is continued to February 13, 2013. Plaintiff¿s request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is granted. Plaintiff demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by January 30, 2013. Supplemental reply, if any, due by February 7, 2013. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PRATT, AVIS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTER et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration And Revocation Of Order On Defendant Ceasars Entertainment Corporations Motion To Quash And To Dismiss For Lack Of Personal Jurisdication (C.C.P. Section 1008(A))
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 24. Plaintiffs¿ motion for reconsideration and revocation of order on defendant Caesars Entertainment Corp.¿s motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is denied. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate any new or different facts, circumstances or law that could not, with reasonable diligence, have been presented in connection with the original motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
